# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.163.0/http/server.ts";

function parseModules(modulesStr: string): string[] {
  return modulesStr.split(',').map(module => module.trim()).filter(Boolean);
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request) {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const params = url.searchParams;
  const modulesFromEnv = Deno.env.get('MODULE_PATHS') || '';
  const modulesFromQuery = params.get('modules') || '';
  const modulePaths = [
    ...parseModules(modulesFromEnv),
    ...parseModules(modulesFromQuery),
  ];

  const results: Record<string, any> = {};

  for (const path of modulePaths) {
    try {
      const module = await import(path);
      results[path] = module.default || module;
    } catch (error) {
      results[path] = { error: error.message };
    }
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(results, null, 2), {
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  });
}

serve(handleRequest);
